sections.title: Foundational Synthesis — Service Classification & End-Market Mapping
required_elements: TIC value chain position (NDT, AIM, certification & compliance, rope access, engineering & consulting)
Revenue mix across service lines and corresponding margin profiles
End-market diversification analysis (oil & gas, power generation, pipeline, aerospace, infrastructure, manufacturing)
End-market concentration risk quantification (top 3 markets % of revenue)
Regulatory demand drivers quantification (PSM, API 510/570/653, PHMSA, NRC, ASME)
Regulatory mandate vs. discretionary spending split

sections.title: Unit Economics — The Labor Leverage Model
required_elements: Revenue per technician ($150K-250K benchmark for NDT Level II, $300K-500K for specialty)
Bill rate vs. cost rate spread (40-60% healthy range)
Technician utilization rate and retention rate assessment (turnover impact quantified)
Certification depth analysis (% holding advanced certifications)
MSA coverage percentage and contract duration visibility
Turnaround/shutdown revenue percentage and volatility impact
Customer retention rate (>90% indicates lock-in)
M&A cadence, integration success rate, and acquisition multiple discipline

sections.title: Competitive Moat Assessment
required_elements: Certification barriers (ASNT, PCN, nuclear qualification timelines)
Customer relationship stickiness assessment (facility knowledge, protocol familiarity)
Geographic density and branch network relative to industrial corridors
Safety record (TRIR vs. industry average) as customer qualification criterion

sections.title: Financial Logic & Valuation
required_elements: Revenue and margin profile benchmarking (3-7% organic + 5-15% M&A growth target)
Gross margin (25-40% target range) and EBITDA margin (12-20% mature)
FCF conversion (60-80% of EBITDA typical)
Platform premium quantification (diversified vs. single-market operator multiples)
EV/EBITDA valuation framework (10-15x quality operators)

sections.title: Pattern Matching & IDP Flagging
required_elements: Organic growth deceleration while acquisition pace accelerates (underlying weakness indicator)
EBITDA margin flat despite revenue growth (integration problems or wage inflation)
Customer concentration exceeding 15% in single account
Technician turnover spiking (service quality degradation leading indicator)
Safety incident trends (TRIR increasing as operational risk signal)

sections.title: Investigation Tracks
required_elements: Labor market dynamics (technician supply/demand, wage inflation, retirement wave)
Regulatory enforcement trends (OSHA/PHMSA tightening or loosening assessment)
M&A pipeline and valuation discipline (specific targets, integration execution quality)

structural_requirements: All sections present with markdown headings
Revenue per technician calculated with certification level breakdown
Bill rate vs. cost rate spread explicitly calculated
Technician utilization and retention rates trending
MSA coverage percentage and average contract duration documented
M&A track record assessed (integration success rate, margin improvement timeline)
End-market concentration calculated (top 3 markets % of revenue)
Regulatory mandate percentage quantified (non-discretionary revenue floor)
Platform premium justified with peer multiple comparison
Investigation tracks include ASNT technician wage surveys, PHMSA integrity program filings, and plant turnaround scheduling sources
